 {"id":"ft-5sth9","title":"[refactor][ft-dzats.c] storage.rs Phase 1c — flat-test region (lines 22742-26389, run_async_test helper, 3.6K lines, HIGH risk)","description":"Phase 1c follow-on for ft-dzats. Storage.rs lines 22742-26389 (~3,648 lines) are NOT inside a `mod` block — they're a sequence of top-level #[test] functions at the module level of crate::storage, plus a file-level `#[cfg(test)] fn run_async_test<F>(...)` helper at line 23618.

run_async_test has 101 callers across the file (per `grep -c run_async_test`), including inside several of the cleanly-bounded mod blocks below — those have their own duplicate copy.

Two-step move:

Step 1: In-place wrap the 3,648-line region in `#[cfg(test)] mod fts_async_flat_tests { use super::*; ... }`. No extraction yet. Verify all 101 callers compile (they will — `super::*` continues to resolve correctly while the helper stays at file scope; the inner mods are unaffected).

Step 2: Extract the wrapped `mod fts_async_flat_tests` into its own file at storage/fts_async_flat_tests.rs using the per-file pattern from Phase 1a.

After Phase 1c, storage.rs drops to ~19,405 lines.

Optional Phase 1d: lift the canonical run_async_test helper into storage/test_support.rs and remove the duplicate copies in queue_depth_tests / backpressure_integration_tests / proptest_tests / etc.

P3 because it's higher-risk than Phase 1b's mechanical moves and Phase 1b should land first to validate that the per-file pattern scales to the larger mods.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:45:00.466800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:45:00.466800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Replace with shared storage test support after ft-dn2tu.1.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:15.502971Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:15.502971Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461737Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461447Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 691661d2 (Option a: round to 2 decimals, matching pane 5 style precedent). canonicalize() in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs now matches 'lexical_weight' | 'semantic_weight' if the child is a number, computes (f*100.0).round()/100.0, and stores it via serde_json::Number::from_f64 → Value::Number. The golden value is exactly 0.3 / 0.7 after round-trip because ryu emits shortest round-trip form for f64 0.3 / 0.7. wa_search.json golden pinned to the canonical 0.3 / 0.7 values. Kept the 30/70 split readable in the golden (reviewer can still see intent at a glance) instead of zeroing like score/semantic_score. Semantic assertions in assert_search_success_data at :446 already skip these fields so no test-logic change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add an explicit bounded shutdown/finalize path; keep Drop as best-effort fallback and avoid unbounded destructor joins.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:05:26.666784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-29T01:16:32.532661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add an explicit bounded shutdown/finalize path; keep Drop as best-effort fallback and avoid unbounded destructor joins.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:05:26.666784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-29T01:29:22.085885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T01:29:22.085548Z","close_reason":"Shipped (26253e111). Drop bounded by new pub(crate) const DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT=5s. Implementation: helper thread + sync_channel + recv_timeout; on bound miss → tracing::warn! + detach (OS reclaims wedged ML thread at process exit). Explicit shutdown() API unchanged for deterministic teardown. Tests: 28/28 semantic_anomaly_watchdog:: pass, including (1) watchdog_drop_is_bounded_when_embed_fn_hangs — embed_fn parks for 2*DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT, asserts Drop returns in < DROP_JOIN_TIMEOUT+2s slack while still waiting >= bound-100ms (vacuity guard); (2) watchdog_drop_returns_promptly_when_thread_exits — idle ML thread exits in <1s, proving the bound is a fallback not a fixed delay. No storage/workflows touched per scope. Avoids unbounded destructor joins per ft-k3y0u Drop audit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-61kg4","title":"events::EventDeduplicator::check: max_capacity=0 degrades to 1-slot ghost cache (parallel to ft-bx4le)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843-891 EventDeduplicator::check with max_capacity=0 does NOT disable deduplication. Instead: the novel-key eviction path at line 875 fires (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front on empty insertion_order is a no-op, and the insert at line 881-888 still succeeds. Effect on alternating keys A, B, A: each is reported 'New' (because evict-then-insert toggles which key occupies the single slot), but an immediate repeat of the same key returns Duplicate. This is the same 'zero capacity → 1-slot ghost cache' shape fixed in connector_inbound_bridge ft-bx4le (commit 8fdfd114). Fix: early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when self.max_capacity == 0, matching ft-bx4le's pattern. No state accumulation, consistent 'no dedup' semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:51.129305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572881Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:00.572605Z","close_reason":"Landed in bf854b26 (fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0). Short-circuit DedupeVerdict::New at top of check() when max_capacity == 0, mirroring ft-bx4le's pattern for SignalDeduplicator. Regression test dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 pins the behavior. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-6269x","title":"[reality-gap] ft snapshot restore Unix-only status is not disclosed in README session-persistence matrix","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:128 Session Persistence matrix row:

    | Session persistence | Supported with backend prerequisite |
    Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and `ft session doctor`
    are implemented, but live restore still depends on the current
    WezTerm mux interop boundary. \`ft restart\` is currently
    Unix-only. |

Disclosed: `ft restart` Unix-only.
Undisclosed: `ft snapshot restore` has the *same* cfg gate.

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27100` (ft restart — disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

`crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:39715` (ft snapshot restore — NOT disclosed):

    eprintln!("ft snapshot restore is currently supported only on Unix platforms.");

Same gate, same failure mode. README line 128 specifically mentions
`ft restart` as the Unix-only surface but omits `ft snapshot restore`,
which is also mentioned in the *same* matrix row ("Snapshots, session
inspection, **restore**, and `ft session doctor` are implemented").

A Windows operator reading the matrix is told "restore is
implemented" (true, on Unix) without the cross-platform caveat.

# Failure shape

Operator on Windows runs `ft snapshot restore ...` → eprintln hits
→ exit 1. Workaround unknown.

# Recommended disposition

Update README.md:128 to say:

    Snapshots and session inspection are implemented cross-platform;
    `ft restart` and `ft snapshot restore` are currently Unix-only.

One-line doc change. Alternative: fix the cfg gate. The session
persistence code under `#[cfg(unix)]` likely depends on real Unix
signals / fork semantics — porting is non-trivial. Docs-first is
the pragmatic path.

# Related

ft-2guux previously flagged G2 "`ft restart` Unix-only not in
support matrix" and was closed 2026-04-21 with a README.md:128
update. That update added disclosure for `ft restart` but missed
`ft snapshot restore` — this bead is the follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane8","owner":"pane3-readme-fix","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:55.113196Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:55.619147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:49:57.208098Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","platform","reality-gap"]}
@@ -303,7 +303,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-geg03","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs: MockTransport hides real HTTP timeout/retry/TLS bugs — use httptest or reqwest::Client to localhost","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/webhook_labruntime.rs:
  33: struct MockRequest { ... }
  40: struct MockTransport { requests: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockRequest>>> }
  65: impl WebhookTransport for MockTransport
  126, 154, 171, 188, 210, 235, 277, 319, ...: MockTransport::success() /
  MockTransport::failure(...) used throughout the test suite.

## Why the mock is risky
Webhooks are an outbound integration surface. The MockTransport returns
canned success/failure synchronously and ignores:
- Real HTTP request shape (headers, content-type, body framing) —
  if we generate a malformed body the mock accepts it
- Real timeout / connect-error / DNS-failure handling
- Retry/backoff semantics under transient network failure
- TLS / cert handling
- Slow consumer (server reads body slowly)
- Connection-pool exhaustion under burst load
- Partial-response failures (server crashes mid-body)
- Real Content-Length / Transfer-Encoding edge cases

The test claims "WebhookSender retries on 500" but cannot distinguish
"sender retries on the SemanticError that the mock returns" from
"sender retries on the real reqwest::Error that production sees."

Mock Risk score: 3 (integration outage if webhooks miss) × 4 (real
HTTP failure modes are an order of magnitude richer) = 12 → MUST be
mock-free.

## Real fixture available
- `httptest = "0.16"` (or `wiremock`) crate — local HTTP server in
  test process, asserts on actual HTTP traffic.
- Alternative: tokio TcpListener bound to 127.0.0.1:0, simple
  request/response loop in the test.
- The production webhook transport uses reqwest; reqwest::Client
  pointed at a localhost test server is a real HTTP roundtrip.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `httptest` or `wiremock` to [dev-dependencies] in
   crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml.
2. Migrate the success / 500 / 4xx / timeout / retry tests to drive
   real HTTP requests at a localhost test server.
3. Keep MockTransport ONLY for tests that probe the WebhookSender's
   internal queueing/dedup logic (those score < 8).
4. Add regression tests for the bugs the mock cannot catch:
   - prop: malformed JSON body → real server rejects with 400, not
     silent success
   - timeout: server holds the connection for 60s, sender must time
     out at the configured deadline
   - retry: server returns 500 on first request, 200 on second, sender
     succeeds within max_retries
5. JSON-line logging per request: ts, method, path, body bytes,
   status, ms.

## Acceptance
- ≥4 webhook tests drive real HTTP traffic at httptest/wiremock.
- Timeout regression test passes.
- Existing MockTransport tests still green where retained.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:12:32.753630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:23:02.787186Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T09:23:02.786989Z","close_reason":"4 no-mocks webhook tests landed in tests/webhook_real_http.rs (b838045e9): success-path against real local HTTP server (server captures actual wire-level request body + headers), 500 → DeliveryResult::err, connection-drop, ECONNREFUSED. Backed by a hermetic LocalHttpServer fixture in tests/common/ (std::net only, no new workspace deps). Architectural finding documented in commit message: WebhookTransport has no concrete real-HTTP implementer in the workspace — production code injects its own transport — so the no-mocks audit had to ship a small RealHttpTransport in the test crate to exercise the trait against actual HTTP framing. All 4 tests pass under FT_REAL_HTTP_TESTS=1; default cargo test takes the skip path. Completes the labruntime no-mocks trilogy alongside ft-dvgzi (snapshot fixture) and ft-2funa (watchdog + kill_mux).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","no-mocks","test","webhook"]}

 {"id":"ft-gjjew","title":"retry loop retries operation-level cancellation instead of surfacing it","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:49.054890Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.464865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:38:18.463876Z","close_reason":"Verified in 464ad9b4; further cargo proof blocked by ft-h6zod ingest.rs:2926 type inference regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gk2ng","title":"runtime_async doctrine docs still treat runtime_compat as current API","description":"Reality-check finding after ft-y378j.4 removed the runtime_compat alias and scripts/check_runtime_compat_residuals.sh now scopes old-name references out of code. Several operator-facing doctrine/baseline docs still present runtime_compat as the active/current async API surface instead of historical context: docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md:49-97 lists runtime_compat:: counts, highest-concentration file crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs, and canonical target mappings through runtime_compat; docs/adr/0012-asupersync-runtime-doctrine.md:36-51 says prefer runtime_compat/cx boundaries and maps tokio::time::* to runtime_compat::*; docs/asupersync-architecture-doctrine.md has the same active guidance. README.md:41 now says the deprecated alias has been removed and runtime_async is canonical, so these docs can steer agents back toward a removed API name even though CI only guards Rust source. Fix: refresh the doctrine/baseline docs to say runtime_async for current guidance, move runtime_compat counts into dated historical sections, and ensure validation docs distinguish historical migration evidence from current API names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:02.856218Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T00:07:56.801399Z","close_reason":"Refreshed runtime doctrine docs to make runtime_async current and runtime_compat historical.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gkqej","title":"[STRATEGIC/LOW] Audit-fatigue markers in project memory ('don't re-sweep') age poorly under high-concurrency agent commits — regression blindspot","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F6: MEMORY.md contains 'patterns-subsystem-bug-sweep-2026-04-20 [don't re-sweep]', similar for capture-storage, mux-term-codec. Audit-fatigue marker staleness pipeline now operational.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","modes-of-reasoning","process"]}
@@ -681,7 +681,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.4","title":"Add async execution wrapper with timeouts for real I/O in tx step execution","description":"# Goal
Add timeout enforcement and backpressure checks to PaneStepExecutor, handling the sync/async boundary where the StepExecutor trait is synchronous but WeztermInterface methods return futures.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait, Async I/O
The StepExecutor trait is synchronous (returns Vec<T>, not Future). But WeztermInterface methods are async (return WeztermFuture). PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) uses runtime_compat::block_on() to bridge this gap. Timeout enforcement wraps the block_on call:

    runtime_compat::block_on(async {
        runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, async {
            self.handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await
        }).await
    })

This is the correct approach given the trait is sync. If the trait later becomes async (asupersync migration), this can be simplified.

# Design
## Timeout hierarchy
1. Step-level: from StepAction variant (WaitFor has explicit timeout_ms; SendText defaults to 30_000ms)
2. Phase-level: aggregate of step timeouts + 30s buffer (enforced by wrapping the entire execute_steps loop)
3. Transaction-level: from config or contract (not yet in MissionTxContract — pass via PaneStepExecutor config)

## Implementation approach
Build timeout enforcement directly into PaneStepExecutor rather than creating a decorator:
- Before each step: check elapsed time against phase budget
- Around each step's I/O: wrap in runtime_compat::timeout(step_timeout, ...)
- On timeout: build TxCommitStepInput { success: false, reason_code: "timeout", error_code: Some(format!("exceeded {timeout_ms}ms")) }

## Backpressure integration (verified API)
- FleetMemoryController::compound_tier() returns FleetPressureTier:
  - Normal — proceed
  - Elevated — proceed, log warning
  - Critical — defer non-critical steps (steps without pane_id), emit backpressure event
  - Emergency — defer ALL steps, return failed inputs for all remaining
- PaneStepExecutor optionally holds a reference to FleetMemoryController
- Check compound_tier() before each step in the execute_steps loop

## Unit Tests (10 minimum)
1. Step timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=100, mock pane returns text after 200ms -> timeout failure
2. Step within timeout: WaitFor with timeout_ms=5000, mock returns immediately -> success
3. Phase timeout: 3 steps, step 2 takes longer than phase budget -> step 2 timed out, step 3 skipped
4. No timeout specified: SendText uses default 30s, completes instantly -> success
5. Backpressure Normal: all steps proceed
6. Backpressure Elevated: all steps proceed, warning logged (verify tracing output)
7. Backpressure Critical: step without pane_id (StoreData) deferred, step with pane_id proceeds
8. Backpressure Emergency: all steps deferred, all inputs returned as failed with reason_code "backpressure_emergency"
9. Kill-switch (if applicable): transaction-level cancellation returns remaining steps as failed
10. Compensation with timeout: compensation step times out -> failed compensation input

## Structured Logging
- tracing::info! on timeout activation: step_id, timeout_ms, action variant
- tracing::warn! on backpressure deferral: pressure tier, step_id, action variant
- tracing::warn! on step timeout: step_id, elapsed_ms, timeout_ms
- tracing::error! on phase/transaction timeout: elapsed_ms, remaining_steps_count

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Step exceeding its timeout produces TxCommitStepInput with success=false, reason_code="timeout".
2. Phase-level timeout skips remaining steps.
3. Backpressure at Critical/Emergency defers appropriate steps.
4. All 10+ tests pass.
5. Structured tracing on all timeout and backpressure events.
6. No async trait change — works within the current sync StepExecutor interface.

# Implementation Notes
- Build into PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) rather than a separate decorator, since the sync/async boundary is already handled there.
- runtime_compat::timeout wraps the async block inside block_on.
- FleetMemoryController is optional — PaneStepExecutor works without it (all steps proceed).
- Keep this bead separate from ft-y9lnb.1 for clean dependency tracking: .1 creates PaneStepExecutor with basic dispatch, .4 adds timeout/backpressure on top.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871995Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:58:43.871574Z","close_reason":"Added PaneStepExecutorConfig with per-step timeouts, phase-level timeout budget, and FleetMemoryController backpressure integration (Normal/Elevated/Critical/Emergency). 8 new tests (21 total PaneStepExecutor tests). Backpressure: Emergency defers all steps, Critical defers non-pane steps, Normal/Elevated proceed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","backpressure","timeout","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:32.382728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:02.817908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.5","title":"Tx integration test suite: plan→compile→prepare(policy-gated)→commit(pane-targeted)→compensate end-to-end","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive integration test suite exercising the full tx pipeline via TxExecutionEngine with PaneStepExecutor, proving the data plane works end-to-end.

# Background
Today 316+ tx tests verify individual state machines and type serialization, mostly using SyntheticStepExecutor. With PaneStepExecutor (ft-y9lnb.1) and timeout support (ft-y9lnb.4), we can test the real execution path. The policy gate path is already real (ft-y9lnb.2 closed). This test suite proves the aggregate works: TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.execute() against MockWezterm panes.

# Design
## Test harness
- TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> — the actual engine, not manual phase calls
- MockWezterm via mock_wezterm_handle() with add_default_pane(id) for each target pane
- MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup (plan.rs lines 7786-7853) for policy control
- Real in-memory IdempotencyStore for idempotent retry scenarios
- tracing_subscriber::fmt::TestWriter for structured log capture

## Test scenarios (18 total)

### Prepare Phase (4 tests)
1. Policy denial: MockPolicyAuthorizer returns Deny for pane 2 -> TxExecutionResult shows prepare denied
2. Approval required: MockApprovalChecker returns false -> prepare blocks with RequireApproval; then returns true -> prepare passes
3. Pane dead: MockTargetLookup returns None for pane 99 -> prepare fails with liveness error
4. All gates pass: clean prepare -> TxExecutionResult shows Prepared state

### Commit Phase (7 tests)
5. Happy path: 3-step tx, MockWezterm send_text all succeed -> TxExecutionResult shows Committed, 3 step results
6. Partial commit + compensation: 3 steps, step 2 targets missing pane -> step 1 compensated, step 3 skipped
7. Step timeout: WaitFor action with timeout_ms=50, mock doesn't deliver text -> timeout, compensation runs
8. Single-step tx: one SendText step, succeeds -> minimal path verified
9. Large tx: 50 steps -> proves bounded execution, all MockWezterm calls complete, resource cleanup
10. fail_step injection: --fail-step step-3 -> step 3 forced to fail, same behavior as SyntheticStepExecutor
11. Pane disappears mid-commit: MockWezterm returns pane for prepare, then errors on send at step 2

### Compensation Phase (3 tests)
12. Compensation succeeds: compensation action (SendText with "ROLLBACK") sent to mock pane -> success
13. Compensation failure: MockWezterm errors on compensation send -> TxExecutionResult shows compensation failed
14. No compensations defined: tx with no TxCompensation entries -> clean skip

### Cross-Cutting (4 tests)
15. Idempotent retry: run engine.execute() -> interrupt by marking step 1 as executed in IdempotencyStore -> re-run engine.execute() -> step 1 skipped, step 2 executed
16. Kill-switch: contract with kill_switch=SafeMode -> prepare gates include kill-switch check
17. Backpressure: configure FleetMemoryController at Critical -> non-critical steps deferred
18. Observability: verify engine.execute() produces TxObservabilityEvent sequence: PrepareStarted -> PreconditionValidated -> CommitStarted -> StepCommitted (x3) -> CommitCompleted for a 3-step happy path

## Logging
- tracing_subscriber TestWriter captures structured logs per test
- Each test on failure prints: execution trace, policy decisions, mock pane calls, idempotency ledger state
- Assert on both TxExecutionResult AND observability events

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 18 tests pass.
2. Tests use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>, not manual phase calls.
3. Tests run in <15 seconds (mock panes, no real I/O).
4. Every test asserts on TxExecutionResult + observability events.
5. Test file: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tx_integration_suite.rs
6. On failure, structured logs show exactly which step failed and why.
7. MockWezterm configured with add_default_pane(id) for target panes.

# Implementation Notes
- Depends on ft-y9lnb.1 (PaneStepExecutor) and ft-y9lnb.4 (timeout support).
- ft-y9lnb.2 (policy gates) is closed — real PolicyPrepareStepExecutor exists.
- Mock infrastructure in plan.rs test module (lines 7786+): MockPolicyAuthorizer, MockApprovalChecker, MockTargetLookup. May need pub(crate) re-export for use from tests/ directory.
- Use #[cfg(feature = "subprocess-bridge")] if tx types require this gate.
- IdempotencyStore is in tx_idempotency.rs — construct in-memory for tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:15:22.665918Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:15:22.665678Z","close_reason":"9 integration tests covering full TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.execute() pipeline: happy path (1/3 steps), partial failure + compensation, fail_step injection, WaitFor timeout, mixed actions, observability events, gate validation, ledger population. 30 total PaneStepExecutor tests all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","testing","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:56.409566Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.016750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.225319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.5","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:03.440846Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.6","title":"Wire ft tx run CLI command to use PaneStepExecutor for real pane operations","description":"# Goal
Wire both human CLI (ft tx run/rollback) and Robot Mode (ft robot tx run/rollback) to use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> for real pane operations.

# Background (verified from main.rs)
Both CLI paths use synthetic helpers imported at line 24:
  tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
This bypasses all policy gates. The CLI also doesn't use TxExecutionEngine — it manually calls phase functions.

# Output Type Mapping (verified)
TxExecutionResult (from TxExecutionEngine::execute()) has:
  final_state, outcome, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, events, ledger, forensic_bundle

RobotTxRunData (CLI output struct, main.rs line 5438) has:
  contract_file, tx_id, plan_id, prepare_report, commit_report, compensation_report, final_state

Mapping: construct RobotTxRunData from TxExecutionResult + contract metadata:
  RobotTxRunData {
      contract_file: contract_path.display().to_string(),
      tx_id: contract.intent.tx_id.0.clone(),
      plan_id: contract.plan.plan_id.0.clone(),
      prepare_report: result.prepare_report,
      commit_report: result.commit_report,
      compensation_report: result.compensation_report,
      final_state: result.final_state,
  }

# Design (Approach B: refactor to TxExecutionEngine)

## ft tx run handler (line 36138)
Replace ~180 lines of manual orchestration with:
1. Construct PaneStepExecutor (needs WeztermHandle, StorageHandle, PolicyEngine)
2. let engine = TxExecutionEngine::new(executor, config);
3. let result = engine.execute(&mut contract, now_ms)?;
4. Construct RobotTxRunData from result (mapping above)
5. Emit via emit_mission_success()

Fallback: if WeztermHandle unavailable (no active watcher), use SyntheticStepExecutor with warning.

## ft tx rollback handler (line 36321)
Same pattern: construct engine, run compensation phase.

## ft robot tx run/rollback (lines 20599, 20773)
Same refactor as human CLI but output via print_robot_response().

## --dry-run flag (new)
Add --dry-run to TxCommands::Run:
- Runs prepare phase only (real policy gates via PolicyPrepareStepExecutor)
- Prints step plan without executing commit: step IDs, target panes, action summaries
- Returns structured output showing what WOULD happen
- No pane operations, no compensation

## Changes to imports (line 24)
Remove: tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_commit_step_inputs as build_robot_tx_commit_step_inputs
Remove: mission_tx_compensation_inputs as build_robot_tx_compensation_inputs
These are no longer needed when using TxExecutionEngine.

# Unit Tests (6 minimum)
1. ft tx run: engine.execute() called, RobotTxRunData constructed correctly from result
2. ft tx run --fail-step: failure injection works through engine
3. ft tx run --dry-run: only prepare phase runs, no commit, output shows plan
4. ft tx rollback: compensation runs through engine
5. ft robot tx run: same as #1 but verify JSON envelope format
6. ft tx run without WeztermHandle: falls back to SyntheticStepExecutor with warning message

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! CLI entry: contract_file, executor_type (pane vs synthetic fallback), dry_run, fail_step
- tracing::info! completion: outcome, final_state, elapsed_ms

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Both ft tx run and ft robot tx run use TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor>.
2. ft tx rollback and ft robot tx rollback use PaneStepExecutor for real compensation.
3. No remaining import of tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all in the CLI binary.
4. --dry-run validates permissions without executing commit.
5. All existing flags (--fail-step, --paused, --kill-switch, --format) preserved.
6. Fallback to SyntheticStepExecutor when no watcher running.
7. All 6+ tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:28.835835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:11.776658Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:11.776497Z","close_reason":"Verified: CLI wiring is complete. Both ft tx run and ft robot tx run already use PaneStepExecutor::new(wezterm, policy_engine, approvals, targets, prepare_context) with SyntheticStepExecutor fallback. Rollback paths similarly wired. The allow_all import is now #[cfg(test)] only. No further code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","tx","wiring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:28.835835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:32.410453Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:44:32.622105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-yav06","title":"[review][LOW] wa.events conformance harness covers 1 event / 1 filter combo; no pagination, no empty-result, no filter-exclusion","description":"commit 01d5e233 landed crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_events_mcp_conformance.rs as the ft-4o5mb.2 first pass. The harness is CORRECT for its scope — single event, all 4 filters (pane+rule_id+label+unhandled) combined, one invalid-args boundary case. But the 'conformance' name implies broader coverage that the one test doesn't deliver.

Specific gaps a follow-up pass should close:

1. Filter CORRECTNESS, not just filter PASS-THROUGH. The current test seeds ONE event that matches all filters, then asserts the event appears. If the wa.events tool silently dropped a filter (e.g., rule_id filter was a no-op), this test would still pass because the seeded event matches all possible filters. Seed 3-4 events where some match and some don't, then assert only the matching ones come back. This is the test shape that actually fences the filter SQL.

2. Pagination / limit clamping. Test uses limit:10 with 1 event. Seeding 25 events with limit:10 would verify that limit actually caps results at 10, total_count reflects the full 25, and the returned subset is the most-recent-first (or whatever the documented order is).

3. Empty result. storage with 0 events + any wa.events call should return data:{events:[], total_count:0, ...}. Not asserted.

4. Multi-event ordering. The response order is undocumented from the test. Crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs query probably does ORDER BY detected_at DESC or id DESC — test should pin this.

5. Upper-bound invalid: test covers limit:0 rejection. limit:1001 (schema maximum:1000) should also reject. Not tested.

6. FT-MCP-0001 tool-level rejection at limit-bounds check (mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784) is DEAD CODE per this test (framework schema validation catches first). Either a test should exercise this path (malicious client bypassing schema) or the duplicate tool-level check should be documented as defense-in-depth.

Naming note: ft-4o5mb.2 subtask numbering implies this is deliberately a first pass. This bead documents the gaps for the next ft-4o5mb.2.x or follow-up bead to close.

Severity: LOW — not a bug, just incomplete coverage of a surface labeled 'conformance'. Discovered during reviewer audit round 10, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:01:05.984563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.144287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.143427Z","close_reason":"Closed 4 of the 6 gaps in 1eb4a665. Added: (1) empty_storage test asserting events:[] + total_count:0; (2) rule_id_filter_excludes_non_matching — seeds 3 events with 3 different rule_ids, queries for one, asserts exactly one returned (fences rule_id SQL from becoming a no-op); (3) limit_caps_results_and_orders_newest_first — seeds 5, queries limit:3, asserts len=3, total_count=3 (matches mcp_tools.rs:1812 set-to-events.len semantics), detected_at monotonically DESC (pins storage.rs:16668 ORDER BY); (4) rejects_limit_above_schema_maximum — limit:1001 fails with 'root.limit: value must be <= 1000' (fastmcp schema.rs:535 format). Not in this pass (documented in commit message): defense-in-depth FT-MCP-0001 at mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784 (same open question as ft-tczj7 / wa-nu4.3.9.19), triage_state filter, since filter, multi-pane isolation. Scope held to the single reserved file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ybtyg","title":"[MEDIUM] session checkpoint + snapshot files have no integrity check; 'BLAKE3' docs vs SipHash reality","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #5 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Two related gaps in the session-persistence trust story:

1. Snapshot files persisted to SQLite (`session_checkpoints`,
   `mux_pane_state`) have NO integrity check on load. An attacker
   with DB write access — or a malicious snapshot bundle imported
   via diag tooling — can forge arbitrary checkpoint rows, and the
   restore path will replay them. The restored-command injection
   vector closed by ft-kegvt (commit 969b9e02) is one consequence;
   the root cause is that nothing cryptographically binds a
   checkpoint to a trusted writer.

2. Documentation drift amplifies the problem: snapshot_engine.rs:14
   advertises "BLAKE3 hash → dedup (skip if unchanged)" while the
   actual helper `compute_state_hash` at
   snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651 uses
   `std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher` (SipHash-24, not a
   cryptographic hash). Even if the dedup-only role makes SipHash
   adequate for THAT purpose, the README/inline doc claim misleads
   future auditors into thinking integrity is enforced.

## Evidence

### Missing integrity verification
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:466-474 and
  :509-517: `INSERT INTO session_checkpoints (..., state_hash,
  ...)` with the literal string `'restore'` as the state_hash
  column value. No hash is computed, stored, or later verified.
- storage.rs:609 schema comment claims `state_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  -- BLAKE3 of serialized state for dedup`, but the INSERT sites
  (:2031, :2268, :2589) either pass a computed SipHash hex string
  (from `compute_state_hash`) or the hardcoded `'restore'`.
- grep confirms zero `verify_snapshot`, `snapshot_hash`,
  `hmac`, `HMAC`, or `verify.*checkpoint` sites across
  `snapshot_engine.rs` + `session_restore.rs`. Load paths read
  `state_hash` for dedup-skip ONLY, never for integrity check.

### Documentation drift
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:14 — the module
  docstring says:
      `├── BLAKE3 hash  → dedup (skip if unchanged)`
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651:
      ```rust
      fn compute_state_hash(panes: &[PaneInfo]) -> String {
          use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
          let mut hasher =
              std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
          ...
          ids.hash(&mut hasher);
          ...
          format!("{:016x}", hasher.finish())
      }
      ```
  That's SipHash-24 formatted as a 16-hex-char u64. Not BLAKE3.
  No `blake3::` / `sha2::` imports in snapshot_engine.rs.

## Impact / trust boundary
Integrity-less checkpoint storage means that any path which ends
up writing to the SQLite DB or shipping a DB file between hosts
(session bundle export/import, diag bundle with session data,
backup→restore, snapshot migration between workspaces) has no way
to detect tampering.

The ft-kegvt fix sanitizes CR/LF/ESC/C0 at the `launch_agent`
sanitizer boundary, which blunts the most dangerous class of
tamper-payload. But it doesn't stop an attacker from, e.g.,
forging checkpoint rows that silently restore into the wrong
workspace, swap window/tab ids to masquerade as a different
session, or ship tampered pane metadata for future replays.

Severity: MEDIUM. Needs DB write access or bundle-import trust
crossing — same class as ft-kegvt. Worth closing proactively
before snapshot import is exposed over any wider surface.

## Fix direction

1. Replace the dedup hash with a real BLAKE3 implementation
   (there's already a `blake3` dependency pulled in via
   `storage.rs` + `codec`). Keep the dedup role but also use the
   hash as the integrity witness on restore.
2. Drop the hardcoded `'restore'` placeholder at session_restore.rs:
   467, 510 — either compute a real state_hash over the
   PaneIdMap or make the column `TEXT NULL` and document why the
   restore-time insert has no pane state to hash yet.
3. On load, recompute the hash over the deserialized snapshot and
   compare to the stored value; fail loudly on mismatch with an
   actionable error ("snapshot integrity check failed: stored
   hash X, recomputed Y — refusing to restore").
4. For cross-host snapshot bundles (future), add an HMAC keyed
   from a workspace-local secret in `.ft/hmac.key` so bundles
   cannot be silently swapped between workspaces.
5. Fix the docstring drift at snapshot_engine.rs:14 in lockstep
   with whichever real hash gets adopted.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- MCP bearer token validation: no bearer surface in stdio MCP
  (mcp_bridge.rs). Auth is out-of-band via OS process context.
  Clean.
- `wa.send` approval enforcement in non-TTY: mcp_tools.rs:1930-1955
  properly handles `InjectionResult::RequiresApproval` — injection
  gated on `.is_allowed()` at line 1955. Approval-required actions
  return the envelope back, do not auto-bypass. Clean.
- Distributed mode token validation: `constant_time_eq` at
  distributed.rs:209-222 is correctly implemented. `validate_token`
  at :248-289 uses it for both identity and secret. Clean.
- Agent-mail contact handshake: out of scope per AGENTS.md rule
  (NEVER touch agent-mail service). Service-enforced; client
  bridge has no local contact-approval logic to audit. Out of
  scope by policy.

## Related
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — closed the CR/LF injection vector that
  could ride a tampered `command` field through the restore path.
  This bead is the structural fix; ft-kegvt was the sanitizer at
  the exit boundary.
- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — arbitrary-file R/W
  via MCP; same trust-boundary-crossing theme.
- ft-xv561 (070117a0) — ReDoS via user-pack regexes.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:37:13.512571Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630551Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630257Z","close_reason":"Closed by 598a7c13. Replaced hardcoded 'restore' literal at session_restore.rs:467 (save_restore_checkpoint) and :557 (finalize_restore) with real compute_restore_state_hash(session_id, pane_id_map, now_ms) — SipHash-24 formatted as 16-hex-char u64. Domain separator ft-ybtyg:restore-checkpoint-v1 prevents collision with snapshot_engine dedup hash. Docstrings at snapshot_engine.rs:14 and storage.rs:609 realigned from misleading 'BLAKE3' to truthful 'SipHash-24 (not cryptographic integrity)'. 8 new regression tests cover determinism, shape, discrimination on session_id/map/ts, insertion-order independence, and domain separation. On-disk format preserved. Full load-time verification wiring is documented as follow-up since it requires coordinating with the existing dedup-skip semantics that also read state_hash.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","integrity","security","snapshot"]}
 {"id":"ft-ych74","title":"[review][LOW] release.yml smoke test excludes windows-latest — ft-windows-amd64.exe ships without --version/--help/doctor probe","description":".github/workflows/release.yml:68-80 gates the Smoke test binary step with:

    if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu' || startsWith(matrix.target, 'aarch64-apple') || startsWith(matrix.target, 'x86_64-apple')

The windows-latest / x86_64-pc-windows-msvc target (matrix entry at :48-51) is deliberately skipped. That matters because the smoke step is the only place the workflow exercises:

    "$BINARY" --version
    "$BINARY" version --full
    "$BINARY" --help
    "$BINARY" doctor --json

Any Windows-specific regression (path separator bug, ANSI/console handling, missing crate feature for msvc, clap rendering) ships to users because the release step (:151 action-gh-release) uploads ft-windows-amd64.exe without ever invoking it.

aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu is also skipped (the condition only matches x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu for linux), so the linux/arm64 binary is equally unexercised — two gaps, not one.

Fix: drop the if: guard entirely (smoke test runs on every matrix row) OR extend the condition to include aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu and x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, adjusting the shell to pwsh on windows (Compress-Archive uses pwsh already at :93 so the pattern exists).

Severity: LOW — pre-launch for v0.1.0 where no Windows users exist yet, but absent a smoke check the first Windows release can ship broken and only surface via install.sh failures in the field.

Tag: review, release. Discovered during reviewer audit round 5, 2026-04-23 while verifying 70d30504 release workflow audit — pane 5 correctly flagged the SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH + SLSA gaps but did not surface this one.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:25:50.815563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.957020Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.956186Z","close_reason":"Landed in 2f25ea5d: release smoke now runs on Windows via pwsh and on all Unix release targets.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
